heading: Imposition of sanctions with respect to Iranian petroleum
label: 8572 Imposition of sanctions with respect to Iranian petroleum

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Stop Harboring Iranian Petroleum Act, also known as the SHIP Act, and not as part of the Comprehensive Iran Sanctions, Accountability, and Divestment Act of 2010 which comprises this chapter.

Delegation of FunctionsMemorandum of President of the United States, § 1, , 89 F.R. 77757, provided:Sept. 13, 2024(a) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of the Treasury, in consultation with the Secretary of State, the functions and authorities vested in the President by sections 3(a), 3(c)(1), and 3(e) of the Stop Harboring Iranian Petroleum Act (Division J of ) (the “SHIP Act”) [, (c)(1), (e)].Public Law 118–5022 U.S.C. 8572(a)(b) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of the Treasury the functions and authorities vested in the President by section 3(c)(2) of the SHIP Act.(c) I hereby delegate to the Secretary of State the functions and authorities vested in the President by sections 3(h) and 3(i) of the SHIP Act.J.R. Biden, Jr.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: On and after the date that is 180 days after , and except as provided in subsection (e)(2), the President shall impose the sanctions described in subsection (c) with respect to each foreign person that the President determines knowingly engaged, on or after , in an activity described in subsection (b).April 24, 2024April 24, 2024

children.label: A
children.text: that is included on the list of specially designated nationals and blocked persons maintained by the Office of Foreign Assets Control of the Department of the Treasury for transporting Iranian crude oil or petroleum products; or

children.label: B
children.text: of which the operator or owner of such vessel otherwise knowingly engages in a significant transaction involving such vessel to transport, offload, or deal in significant transactions in condensate, refined, or unrefined petroleum products, or other petrochemical products originating from the Islamic Republic of Iran;

owns or operates a vessel through which such owner knowingly conducts a ship to ship transfer involving a significant transaction of any petroleum product originating from the Islamic Republic of Iran;

owns or operates a refinery through which such owner knowingly engages in a significant transaction to process, refine, or otherwise deal in any petroleum product originating from the Islamic Republic of Iran;
is a covered family member of a foreign person described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3); or
is owned or controlled by a foreign person described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3), and knowingly engages in an activity described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3).

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to a vessel described in subsection (b)(1)(A), for a period of not more than 2 years beginning on the date on which the President imposes sanctions with respect to a related foreign port described in subsection (b)(1)(A); and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to a vessel described in subsection (b)(1)(B), for a period of not more than 2 years.

children.heading: Blocking of property
children.label: 2 Blocking of property
children.text: The President shall exercise all of the powers granted to the President under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( et seq.) to the extent necessary to block and prohibit all transactions in property and interests in property of the foreign person if such property and interests in property are in the United States, come within the United States, or are or come within the possession or control of a United States person.50 U.S.C. 1701

children.heading: Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
children.label: 3 Ineligibility for visas, admission, or parole
i
children.text: inadmissible to the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: ineligible to receive a visa or other documentation to enter the United States; and

children.label: iii
children.text: otherwise ineligible to be admitted or paroled into the United States or to receive any other benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act ( et seq.).8 U.S.C. 1101

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An alien described in subsection (a) is subject to revocation of any visa or other entry documentation regardless of when the visa or other entry documentation is or was issued.

children.heading: Immediate effect
children.label: ii Immediate effect
children.text: A revocation under clause (i) shall take effect immediately and automatically cancel any other valid visa or entry documentation that is in the alien’s possession.

children.label: i
children.text: to permit the United States to comply with the Agreement regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations, signed at Lake Success , and entered into force , between the United Nations and the United States, or other applicable international obligations; orJune 26, 1947November 21, 1947

children.label: ii
children.text: to carry out or assist law enforcement activity in the United States.

children.heading: Penalties
children.label: 4 Penalties
children.text: The penalties provided for in subsections (b) and (c) of section 206 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act () shall apply to a person that violates, attempts to violate, conspires to violate, or causes a violation of this section or any regulations promulgated to carry out this section to the same extent that such penalties apply to a person that commits an unlawful act described in section 206(a) of that Act.50 U.S.C. 1705

For purposes of determinations under subsection (a) that a foreign person engaged in activities described in subsection (b), a foreign person shall not be determined to know that petroleum or petroleum products originated from Iran if such person relied on a certificate of origin or other documentation confirming that the origin of the petroleum or petroleum products was a country other than Iran, unless such person knew or had reason to know that such documentation was falsified.
Nothing in this subchapter shall be construed to affect the availability of any existing authorities to issue waivers, exceptions, exemptions, licenses, or other authorization.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President may exercise all authorities under sections 203 and 205 of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act ( and 1704) for purposes of carrying out this section.50 U.S.C. 1702

children.heading: Deadline for regulations
children.label: 2 Deadline for regulations
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the President shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary for the implementation of this subchapter.April 24, 2024

children.label: A
children.text: the proposed regulations; and

children.label: B
children.text: the specific provisions of this subchapter that the regulations are implementing.

children.label: A
children.text: the conduct or facilitation of a transaction for the provision of agricultural commodities, food, medicine, medical devices, or humanitarian assistance, or for humanitarian purposes; or

children.label: B
children.text: transactions that are necessary for or related to the activities described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Agricultural commodity
children.label: A Agricultural commodity
children.text: The term “agricultural commodity” has the meaning given that term in .section 5602 of title 7

children.heading: Medical device
children.label: B Medical device
children.text: The term “medical device” has the meaning given the term “device” in .section 321 of title 21

children.heading: Medicine
children.label: C Medicine
children.text: The term “medicine” has the meaning given the term “drug” in .section 321 of title 21

children.heading: Exception for safety of vessels and crew
children.label: g Exception for safety of vessels and crew
children.text: Sanctions under this section shall not apply with respect to a person providing provisions to a vessel otherwise subject to sanctions under this section if such provisions are intended for the safety and care of the crew aboard the vessel, the protection of human life aboard the vessel, or the maintenance of the vessel to avoid any environmental or other significant damage.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The President may, on a case-by-case basis and for periods not to exceed 180 days each, waive the application of sanctions imposed with respect to a foreign person under this section if the President certifies to the appropriate congressional committees, not later than 15 days after such waiver is to take effect, that the waiver is vital to the national interests of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: is no longer engaging in activities described in subsection (b); or

children.label: B
children.text: has taken and is continuing to take significant, verifiable steps toward permanently terminating such activities.

children.label: A
children.text: ;section 4813(c)(1)(A) of title 50

children.label: B
children.text: ;section 2371 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: ; orsection 2780 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: any other provision of law; and
Iran has ceased the pursuit, acquisition, and development of, and verifiably dismantled, its nuclear, biological, and chemical weapons, ballistic missiles, and ballistic missile launch technology.
